meta.title: Enactome
meta.subtitle: A connectome-constrained simulation of the Drosophila brain

scenes.heading: ENACTOME
scenes.sub: Turning the fly brain wiring diagram into a working simulation
scenes.caption: The fruit fly Drosophila has about 140,000 neurons. Every connection between them has now been mapped.

scenes.heading: The connectome
scenes.caption: A connectome is the full wiring diagram of a brain. On its own it is a static map. Enactome turns it into a model that computes.
We load the connectome into the engine. Each synapse is given a sign from its neurotransmitter: excitatory or inhibitory.
The olfactory pathway: odor receptors to projection neurons to the lateral horn, the circuit for innate smell-driven behavior.
The registry runs 35 experiments, each recreating a published finding from the connectome. All pass.
The lateral horn compresses the odor code and is biased toward aversion: aversive output neurons outnumber appetitive ones.
A gene-to-cell-type layer links circuits to human disease. Fly orthologs of epilepsy and Parkinson genes are enriched in neurons.
The perturbation engine predicts the behavioral effect of silencing a neuron type, and names the genetic driver to test it.

scenes.heading: The paper
scenes.caption: Each result is a figure in the manuscript.
The manuscript: the platform, the lateral horn, learning and neuromodulation, whole-brain integration, the visual system, and disease genetics.

scenes.heading: ENACTOME
scenes.sub: One connectome. Thirty-five reproducible experiments. From wiring to computation.
